title: Node timers/promises safe fulfilled payload recovery
summary: Reuse the imported PromiseSettledValue capability for only the safe no-options payload arities of Node timers/promises. setTimeout(delay, value) can name argument 1 as the fulfilled payload, and setImmediate(value) can name argument 0. Option-bearing arities remain domain-only because options.signal can reject.

direct_named_binding_source_scan.description: Read-only scan of JS/TS files that first identifies named bindings from node:timers/promises or timers/promises, including conservative const CommonJS destructuring, then counts calls to the local binding names by top-level arity.

corpus.note: The direct source scan is used only to price the newly opened safe-payload arities. It is not a replacement for the prior 97-call domain slice, which was recorded by earlier artifacts.

opened_capability: LibraryImportedPromiseFactoryContract can now declare an optional fulfilled payload argument.
Dependency-backed node:timers/promises and timers/promises setTimeout calls at exactly two arguments emit fulfilled PromiseSettledValue for argument 1.
Dependency-backed node:timers/promises and timers/promises setImmediate calls at exactly one argument emit fulfilled PromiseSettledValue for argument 0.
The PromiseSettledValue evidence depends on the admitted LibraryApi occurrence evidence, which itself depends on imported binding proof.

Promise continuation recovery still requires the existing PromiseLike receiver proof, admitted Promise continuation API evidence, imported call-target identity, and non-thenable-safe fulfilled payload proof.
closed_boundaries: setTimeout(delay) and setImmediate() remain domain-only because they have no explicit payload node to name.
setTimeout(delay, value, options) and setImmediate(value, options) remain domain-only because options.signal can reject with AbortError.
Possible-thenable fulfilled payloads remain opaque even when PromiseSettledValue evidence names the payload.
scheduler.wait, scheduler.yield, setInterval async iteration, namespace/default imports, mutable or dynamic CommonJS shapes, and broad scheduling equivalence remain closed.
The current pinned corpus has zero direct safe-payload call sites, so this slice intentionally reports zero corpus recall delta.
soundness_notes: The implementation uses the shared imported Promise settled-value evidence path rather than a Node-timers-specific value-graph shortcut.
The value graph keeps the recovered continuation result behind a Promise boundary; it does not converge with a synchronous payload expression.
The hard-negative test covers option-bearing timer calls so AbortSignal rejection cannot be erased.
The hard-negative test covers possible thenable payloads so Promise assimilation risk remains fail-closed.

performance_check.command: nose query bench/repos/execa all top=0 --mode semantic --format json
performance_check.semantic_output_diff: byte-for-byte identical on the final measured run

source_scan_commands: node read-only binding/arity scan over bench/repos JS/TS files for named imports and conservative const require destructuring from node:timers/promises or timers/promises
